text.title: CRO Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Concrete CRO wins demonstrating the research-first approach, plus antipatterns that invalidate test results.

example.p: Research: heatmaps showed hesitation at password field; session recordings showed confusion about requirements; exit surveys cited "too many fields". Hypothesis: reducing to email-only signup (set password later) will increase form completion because it removes the most-cited friction point. Result: 45% lift, no negative impact on activation.

example.note: Pricing page: 2% → 3.3% trial conversion over two sequential tests.
example.p: Research: users wanted easy plan comparison; FAQ questions revealed feature confusion; mobile layout was broken. Three tests: (1) comparison table — no impact; (2) FAQ section added — +15%; (3) mobile redesign — +20% on mobile segment. All three run to full significance; mobile segmented separately from desktop.

antipattern.description: Stopping tests early when results look good at 80% confidence.
antipattern.reason: At 80% confidence the false-positive rate is ~30%, not 5%. "We have a winner" declared on day 4 pollutes the learning library with phantom gains.

antipattern.description: Using heatmaps as the only research source before forming hypotheses.
antipattern.reason: Heatmaps reveal where users click but not why they leave. Pair with session recordings and exit surveys to triangulate intent; heatmap-only hypotheses have a lower win rate.

antipattern.description: Copying "best practice" changes from industry case studies without local research.
antipattern.reason: Urgency timers and social-proof-above-fold tactics fail to replicate without local data confirming the same friction exists in your specific audience.

antipattern.description: Running a major redesign as an A/B test.
antipattern.reason: Multivariate changes cannot isolate which element caused any measured effect; use user research and prototype testing instead.

antipattern.description: No learning library entry after a test concludes — win or loss.
antipattern.reason: Without documentation, teams re-run the same failed tests and lose accumulated institutional knowledge. The library is the primary long-term asset of a CRO program.
